NOTICE OF RESCHEDULED EARLY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE - EARLY SETTLEMENT PROGRAM

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CENTURY SURETY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. CENTURY'S MOVING PAPERS DO NOT ESTABLISH AS AN UNDISPUTED FACT THAT THE FIRE WAS CAUSED BY "ANY OTHER HEATED SUBSTANCE." TWO CAUSES ARE UNDISPUTED - THE MISUSE OF [RADOYCIS'] TORCH AND HIS FAILURE TO CONDUCT A PROPER "FIRE WATCH". (FACT 10.) ALTHOUGH WHEN MODIFIED BITUMEN IS HEATED IT BECOMES MOLTEN, CENTURY HAS NOT POSITED AS AN UNDISPUTED FACT THAT THE HEATED BITUMEN CAUSED THE FIRE. TO THE EXTENT CENTURY SEEKS TO HAVE THE COURT DETERMINE THAT "THE MISUSE OF [RADOYCIS'] TORCH IN PERFORMING 'TORCH DOWN ROOFING' CONSTITUTES "ANY OTHER HEATED SUBSTANCE", THE COURT FINDS THAT THE POLICY LANGUAGE IS AMBIGUOUS. ONCE THAT FINDING IS MADE, DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS PRESENTED FACTS THAT RADOYCIS' REASONABLE EXPECTATION WAS THAT THE "ANY OTHER HEATED SUBSTANCE" EXCLUSION DID NOT EXCLUDE THE CONDUCT AT ISSUE. THERE ALSO ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE APPLICATION CONSTITUTES A MISREPRESENTATION FACT BASED ON THE DECLARATIONS OF RADOYCIS AND PETERSON. THE COURT GRANTS CENTURY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE BAD FAITH CAUSE OF ACTION. CENTURY'S INTERPRETATION OF THE POLICY AS TO THESE FACTS IS NOT UNREASONABLE AS A MATTER OF LAW. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Christopher Wagner, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, 213-576-5000 for plaintiff and moving party Century Surety Co.
Timothy Wilson, Esq., 415-438-7802 for defendant and opposing party San Carlos Roofing

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
PLAINTIFF CENTURY SURETY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Century's moving papers do not establish as an undisputed fact that the fire was caused by "any other heated substance." Two causes are undisputed - the misuse of [Radoycis'] torch and his failure to conduct a proper "fire watch". (Fact 10.) Although when modified bitumen is heated it becomes molten, Century has not posited as an undisputed fact that the heated bitumen caused the fire. To the extent Century seeks to have the Court determine that "the misuse of [Radoycis'] torch in performing 'torch down roofing' constitutes "any other heated substance", the Court finds that the policy language is ambiguous. Once that finding is made, defendant/cross-complainant has presented facts that Radoycis' reasonable expectation was that the "any other heated substance" exclusion did not exclude the conduct at issue.
There also are triable issues of fact as to whether the application constitutes a misrepresentation fact based on the Declarations of Radoycis and Peterson.
The Court grants Century's motion for summary adjudication of the bad faith cause of action. Century's interpretation of the policy as to these facts is not unreasonable as a matter of
law. The Court will issue its ruling on the evidentiary objections at the hearing. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)
